_comment: PPTX Release Notes spec template. Fill in each section to generate a branded presentation. Bullets support 'text' (primary) and optional 'detail' (secondary gray text). Do NOT include JIRA issue keys.

executive_summary.title: Delegated Requirements Traceability
executive_summary.description: New capability: cross-product requirement delegation tracked end-to-end in PICASso

executive_summary.title: Data Protection & Privacy — Maturity Visibility
executive_summary.description: Compliance clarity: unreviewed sections now explicitly flagged to reviewers

executive_summary.title: Defect Fixes
executive_summary.description: Quality improvements across core requirements, BackOffice, CSRR, and export functions

features.title: Delegated Requirements Traceability
features.subtitle: Eliminates compliance blind spots: delegation details that previously lived in emails are now formally captured and traceable in PICASso.

bullets.text: Previously, requirements delegated to partner products had no formal record in PICASso
Teams tracked delegation in emails and spreadsheets — creating audit and compliance risk
Now: each delegation is recorded with target product, release, requirement, due date, and scope covered
Status and Evaluation Status of the delegated-to requirement update automatically — no manual chasing
Traceability is bidirectional — visible from both the delegating and delegated-to requirement
Collapsible section on the delegated-to requirement lists all dependants with status and due dates
Warning surfaces at release submission if delegation details are incomplete
Release manager is informed before moving to the next stage — submission is not blocked
Delegation details propagate automatically when a release is cloned
Zero re-entry overhead for next release cycle; both directions ('Delegated to' and 'From') are copied

features.screenshot_caption: Edit Requirement pop-up: 'Delegated' status selected, showing delegation fields — Product Name, Release, Requirement, Due Date

features.title: Flexible Delegation Input — Three Methods
features.subtitle: Designed for fast adoption at any team size: individual entry, bulk operations, and Excel upload all supported from day one.

bullets.text: Manual — open any requirement, select 'Delegated' to reveal the delegation form
bullets.detail: Supports multiple target products and requirements per delegated item via '+Add' buttons; each removable

bullets.text: Bulk Actions — delegate multiple requirements simultaneously in a single operation
bullets.detail: Overwrite protection: confirmation required if any selected requirements already have delegation details

bullets.text: Excel import — new 'Delegation Details' tab in the exported XLSX, pre-populated where data exists
bullets.detail: Supports multi-product and multi-requirement rows per requirement; 'Instructions' tab provides step-by-step guidance

bullets.text: Full import validation prevents bad data from entering the system
bullets.detail: Checks: product ID format, requirement scope for release, date format, duplicates, deactivated references

features.screenshot_caption: Bulk Actions delegation form: multiple requirements selected, delegation fields visible, overwrite confirmation pop-up

features.title: Delegation Visibility Across the Entire Platform
features.subtitle: Delegation status is surfaced wherever teams work — requirements, SDL, privacy reviews, and Jira/Jama — with full audit trail.

bullets.text: Delegation fields visible in Requirements View, Edit pop-ups, CSRR tab, and all DPPR tab sections
bullets.detail: All linked values are clickable — one click navigates to the delegated entity in read-only mode

bullets.text: Jira / Jama sync: warning icon flags 'Delegated' status set via sync without delegation details filled
bullets.detail: Warning re-surfaced at release stage submission so nothing slips through the workflow unnoticed

bullets.text: Complete audit trail: 'Delegation Details Update' activity logged in release history
bullets.detail: Every add, change, or removal — with timestamp and user — filterable in the 'Filter by Activity' dropdown

bullets.text: Delegation context available during formal SDL and Privacy review sessions
bullets.detail: CSRR and DPPR tabs display both the delegating requirement's details and the delegated-to requirement's collapsible section

features.screenshot_caption: Release history log showing 'Delegation Details Update' entry with filter dropdown; Jira warning icon visible on requirement row

features.title: Data Protection & Privacy — Maturity Level Clarity
features.subtitle: Privacy review status is now unambiguous: a section showing a maturity level has always been reviewed; 'Not Yet Evaluated' means it has not.

bullets.text: Previous behaviour: sections with only an Expected Maturity level appeared to be reviewed
bullets.detail: Auto-filled default value created false confidence — unreviewed sections were indistinguishable from reviewed ones

bullets.text: New behaviour: 'Not Yet Evaluated' stamp shown by default until a reviewer manually saves a level
bullets.detail: Applies to all applicable sections not marked 'Not Applicable' where no manual Maturity level has been set

bullets.text: Once saved, the stamp updates immediately to show the maturity level name selected by the reviewer
bullets.detail: No page refresh required; change is visible to all users in real time across the Privacy Section sidebar

bullets.text: Visual arrows show alignment with the Expected Maturity: ↑ above / ↓ below / no arrow = on target
bullets.detail: Enables instant scan of compliance posture — reviewers see at a glance which sections need attention

features.screenshot_caption: Privacy Section sidebar: left shows 'Not Yet Evaluated' stamp (unreviewed); right shows maturity level name with alignment arrow (reviewed)

defect_fixes.subtitle: Fixes span requirements management, data integrity, BackOffice configuration, and export accuracy
fixes: Requirements no longer duplicated in releases or submitted to Jama as duplicates
Must/Should column restored in exported Product Requirements XLSX
DPP Risk value now correctly cloned when a release is copied
Closed actions no longer block editing of other actions in the same section
CSRR: Summary field text now fully displayed (was truncated to one line)
Limited-scope users can no longer see unauthorized Organizations in filters
Approver role now correctly recorded in multi-approver release history stages
BackOffice: Excel template upload error message is now accurate and actionable
BackOffice: Inactive API consumer records can now be reactivated without errors
BackOffice: 'Add Severity Input' in SCA Tool List works without errors

key_benefits.title: Formal Delegation Trail — End to End
key_benefits.description: Requirements delegated to partner products or external teams are now formally captured in PICASso. Delegation details — product, release, requirement, due date, and scope — replace email chains and spreadsheets that previously created audit and compliance exposure.

key_benefits.title: Risk Surfaced Before It Escalates
key_benefits.description: Delegation warnings surface at every stage submission. Jira/Jama sync flags missing details in real time. Bidirectional traceability shows exactly which products depend on each requirement — issues are caught at the gate, not after the fact.

key_benefits.title: Unambiguous Privacy Review Status
key_benefits.description: 'Not Yet Evaluated' replaces misleading auto-filled maturity values. Reviewers instantly see which Privacy sections still need their attention. Arrow indicators show alignment with Expected Maturity at a glance — eliminating false confidence in compliance posture.

key_benefits.title: Less Manual Work Per Release Cycle
key_benefits.description: Bulk delegation, Excel import with full validation, and automatic propagation on release clone reduce manual overhead. Delegation details set once are carried forward — no re-entry required for the next cycle.
Defects fixed: · Requirements management  ·  BackOffice configuration  ·  CSRR & export accuracy  ·  Data integrity & access control
